21812199226.name: Zwift - Castle Crit in Makuri Islands
21812199226.highlight: **Short, Sharp Effort.** You delivered a high-intensity, short-duration effort with a peak power output of **270W**.
21812199226.was: This session was a focused burst of power. Your Normalized Power of **170W** suggests you were consistently operating at a challenging level for the duration. The relatively low duration (13 minutes) indicates this was a targeted effort, likely focused on improving your anaerobic capacity and peak power. Your cadence held steady at **71 spm**, which is typical for your shorter, higher-intensity Zwift sessions.
21812199226.better_next: Incorporate similar short bursts into your training week, but experiment with slightly longer durations (15-20 minutes) to build sustained power.

21812053328.name: Zwift - Race:  Stage 5 - Race - Tour de Zwift (D) on Ocean Lava Cliffside Loop in Watopia
21812053328.highlight: **Race Day Power.** You put down a strong performance in a Zwift race, achieving an average power of **234W** and a max of **561W**.
21812053328.was: This race clearly demanded a significant output. Your average heart rate of **170 bpm** confirms you were pushing into a high-intensity zone for the entire 34-minute duration. The substantial difference between your average and maximum power (**561W**) indicates you were effectively utilizing surges and responding to race dynamics. Your cadence of **77 spm** remained consistent, suggesting good efficiency under pressure. This is currently your longest ride of the year at **12.22 miles**.
21812053328.better_next: Analyze the race data further to identify key moments where you lost or gained ground. Focus on replicating successful tactics and addressing areas for improvement in future races.

21781846223.name: Zwift - Group Ride: Standard - Stage 5 - Tour de Zwift on Glyph Heights in Watopia
21781846223.highlight: **Endurance and Elevation.** You completed a long group ride with significant elevation gain (**2021 ft**), maintaining a solid average power of **210W**.
21781846223.was: This ride was a substantial endurance effort. The 70-minute duration, combined with the elevation profile, demanded consistent power output and aerobic capacity. Your average cadence of **76 spm** suggests you found a comfortable and efficient rhythm for the climb. Your average heart rate of **162 bpm** indicates you were working at a challenging, but sustainable, intensity. This is a good indicator of your current aerobic base.
21781846223.better_next: Incorporate more structured interval training into your week to further enhance your aerobic power and climbing speed.

21767518512.name: Treadmill Running
21767518512.highlight: **High Cadence Run.** You maintained a remarkably high running cadence of **155 spm** during your 5-mile treadmill run.
21767518512.was: Your cadence is the standout metric here. While **155 spm** is excellent, it's worth investigating *why* it's so high. Was this a conscious effort to shorten your stride, or was it a natural response to the treadmill? Your pace of **7:54/mi** is consistent with your recent baseline, but the high cadence suggests a potential shift in running mechanics. Your heart rate of **168 bpm** is elevated for that pace, which could indicate fatigue or a need to focus on relaxed running form. This is a pattern we've seen in your recent treadmill sessions.
21767518512.better_next: Experiment with slightly lowering your cadence while maintaining the same pace, focusing on lengthening your stride and improving running economy. Monitor your heart rate response to see if you can achieve the same pace with less effort.

21739333767.name: Zwift - Race:  Stage 4 - Race - Tour de Zwift (D) on London 8 in London
21739333767.highlight: **Competitive Power Output.** You delivered a strong race performance with an average power of **228W** and a peak of **527W**.
21739333767.was: This race demanded a significant power output, as evidenced by your average heart rate of **170 bpm**. Your cadence of **82 spm** is slightly higher than your typical race cadence, potentially indicating a strategy to maintain speed on the course's varied terrain. The substantial peak power suggests you were able to respond effectively to attacks and surges. Your pace of **19.8 mph** is competitive.
21739333767.better_next: Review the race profile and identify sections where you could have optimized your pacing or power output.

21733443554.name: Zwift - Group Ride: Long - Stage 4 - Tour de Zwift on Triple Loops in London
21733443554.highlight: **Long Ride Endurance.** You completed a substantial 26.56-mile Zwift group ride, demonstrating excellent endurance.
21733443554.was: This ride was a significant test of your aerobic base. Maintaining an average power of **198W** for 88 minutes requires consistent effort and pacing. The elevation gain of **1900 ft** added to the challenge, demanding sustained climbing power. Your average cadence of **78 spm** suggests you found a comfortable rhythm for the duration.
21733443554.better_next: Consider incorporating more structured interval training into your week to further enhance your aerobic power and climbing speed.
